class Group(Model):
    """
    Aggregated message which summarizes a set of Events.
    """

    __relocation_scope__ = RelocationScope.Excluded

    project = FlexibleForeignKey("sentry.Project")
    logger = models.CharField(
        max_length=64, blank=True, default=str(DEFAULT_LOGGER_NAME), db_index=True
    )
    level = BoundedPositiveIntegerField(
        choices=[(key, str(val)) for key, val in sorted(LOG_LEVELS.items())],
        default=logging.ERROR,
        blank=True,
        db_index=True,
    )
    message = models.TextField()
    culprit = models.CharField(
        max_length=MAX_CULPRIT_LENGTH, blank=True, null=True, db_column="view"
    )
    num_comments = BoundedPositiveIntegerField(default=0, null=True)
    platform = models.CharField(max_length=64, null=True)
    status = BoundedPositiveIntegerField(
        default=GroupStatus.UNRESOLVED,
        choices=(
            (GroupStatus.UNRESOLVED, _("Unresolved")),
            (GroupStatus.RESOLVED, _("Resolved")),
            (GroupStatus.IGNORED, _("Ignored")),
        ),
        db_index=True,
    )
    substatus = BoundedIntegerField(
        null=True,
        choices=(
            (GroupSubStatus.UNTIL_ESCALATING, _("Until escalating")),
            (GroupSubStatus.ONGOING, _("Ongoing")),
            (GroupSubStatus.ESCALATING, _("Escalating")),
            (GroupSubStatus.UNTIL_CONDITION_MET, _("Until condition met")),
            (GroupSubStatus.FOREVER, _("Forever")),
        ),
    )
    times_seen = BoundedPositiveIntegerField(default=1, db_index=True)
    last_seen = models.DateTimeField(default=timezone.now, db_index=True)
    first_seen = models.DateTimeField(default=timezone.now, db_index=True)
    first_release = FlexibleForeignKey("sentry.Release", null=True, on_delete=models.PROTECT)
    resolved_at = models.DateTimeField(null=True, db_index=True)
    # active_at should be the same as first_seen by default
    active_at = models.DateTimeField(null=True, db_index=True)
    time_spent_total = BoundedIntegerField(default=0)
    time_spent_count = BoundedIntegerField(default=0)
    # deprecated, do not use. GroupShare has superseded
    is_public = models.BooleanField(default=False, null=True)
    data = LegacyTextJSONField(null=True)
    short_id = BoundedBigIntegerField(null=True)
    type = BoundedPositiveIntegerField(
        default=DEFAULT_TYPE_ID, db_default=DEFAULT_TYPE_ID, db_index=True
    )
    priority = models.PositiveIntegerField(null=True)
    priority_locked_at = models.DateTimeField(null=True)
    seer_fixability_score = models.FloatField(null=True)
    seer_autofix_last_triggered = models.DateTimeField(null=True)
    # This actually represents the last timestamp when the explorer agent completes a step
    seer_explorer_autofix_last_triggered = models.DateTimeField(null=True)

    objects: ClassVar[GroupManager] = GroupManager(cache_fields=("id",))

    class Meta:
        app_label = "sentry"
        db_table = "sentry_groupedmessage"
        verbose_name_plural = _("grouped messages")
        verbose_name = _("grouped message")
        permissions = (("can_view", "Can view"),)
        indexes = [
            models.Index(fields=("project", "first_release")),
            models.Index(fields=("project", "id")),
            models.Index(fields=("project", "status", "last_seen", "id")),
            models.Index(fields=("project", "status", "type", "last_seen", "id")),
            models.Index(fields=("project", "status", "substatus", "last_seen", "id")),
            models.Index(fields=("project", "status", "substatus", "type", "last_seen", "id")),
            models.Index(fields=("project", "status", "substatus", "id")),
            models.Index(fields=("status", "substatus", "id")),  # TODO: Remove this
            models.Index(fields=("status", "substatus", "first_seen")),
            models.Index(fields=("project", "status", "priority", "last_seen", "id")),
        ]
        unique_together = (("project", "short_id"),)

    __repr__ = sane_repr("project_id")

    def __str__(self) -> str:
        return f"({self.times_seen}) {self.title}"

    def save(self, *args, **kwargs):
        if not self.last_seen:
            self.last_seen = timezone.now()
        if not self.first_seen:
            self.first_seen = self.last_seen
        if not self.active_at:
            self.active_at = self.first_seen
        # We limit what we store for the message body
        self.message = strip(self.message)
        if self.message:
            self.message = truncatechars(self.message.splitlines()[0], 255)
        if self.times_seen is None:
            self.times_seen = 1
        super().save(*args, **kwargs)

    def get_absolute_url(
        self,
        params: Mapping[str, str] | None = None,
        event_id: str | None = None,
    ) -> str:
        # Built manually in preference to django.urls.reverse,
        # because reverse has a measured performance impact.
        organization = self.organization

        if self.issue_category == GroupCategory.FEEDBACK:
            path = f"/organizations/{organization.slug}/feedback/"
            params = {
                **(params or {}),
                "feedbackSlug": f"{self.project.slug}:{self.id}",
                "project": str(self.project.id),
            }
            query = urlencode(params)
            return organization.absolute_url(path, query=query)
        else:
            path = f"/organizations/{organization.slug}/issues/{self.id}/"
            if event_id:
                path += f"events/{event_id}/"
            query = None
            if params:
                query = urlencode(params)
            return organization.absolute_url(path, query=query)

    def get_absolute_api_url(self):
        path = f"/issues/{self.id}/"
        return self.organization.absolute_api_url(path=path)

    @property
    def qualified_short_id(self):
        if self.short_id is not None:
            return f"{self.project.slug.upper()}-{base32_encode(self.short_id)}"

    def is_over_resolve_age(self):
        resolve_age = self.project.get_option("sentry:resolve_age", None)
        if not resolve_age:
            return False
        return self.last_seen < timezone.now() - timedelta(hours=int(resolve_age))

    def is_ignored(self):
        return self.get_status() == GroupStatus.IGNORED

    def is_unresolved(self):
        return self.get_status() == GroupStatus.UNRESOLVED

    def is_resolved(self):
        return self.get_status() == GroupStatus.RESOLVED

    def has_replays(self):
        def make_snuba_params_for_replay_count_query():
            return SnubaParams(
                organization=self.project.organization,
                projects=[self.project],
                user=None,
                start=datetime.now() - timedelta(days=14),
                end=datetime.now(),
                environments=[],
                teams=[],
            )

        def _cache_key(issue_id) -> str:
            return f"group:has_replays:{issue_id}"

        from sentry.replays.usecases.replay_counts import get_replay_counts
        from sentry.search.events.types import SnubaParams

        metrics.incr("group.has_replays")

        # XXX(jferg) Note that this check will preclude backend projects from receiving the "View Replays"
        # link in their notification. This will need to be addressed in a future change.
        if not self.project.flags.has_replays:
            metrics.incr("group.has_replays.project_has_replays_false")
            return False

        cached_has_replays = cache.get(_cache_key(self.id))
        if cached_has_replays is not None:
            metrics.incr(
                "group.has_replays.cached",
                tags={
                    "has_replays": cached_has_replays,
                },
            )
            return cached_has_replays

        data_source = (
            Dataset.Discover
            if self.issue_category == GroupCategory.ERROR
            else Dataset.IssuePlatform
        )

class WorkflowEventData:
    event: GroupEvent | Activity
    group: Group
    group_state: GroupState | None = None
    # True when an issue transitions to the ESCALATING substatus for any reason.
    has_escalated: bool | None = None
    workflow_env: Environment | None = None

    # The cache field is used to deduplicate repeated work within the context

I now have a complete structural picture from the Sense index. Here is the audit, worked step by step.

---

## Step 1 — Orientation

**What this is:** Sentry — a Django (Python backend) + React (TypeScript frontend) error-monitoring platform. ~17k files, ~129k symbols. Not a multi-package monorepo in the npm sense; it's one large Django app plus a React SPA.

**How the code is organized:**
- `src/sentry/` — the backend Django app (~40k symbols). Sub-trees that matter here: `models/` (ORM), `api/` + `issues/endpoints/` (REST layer), `tasks/` (Celery), `integrations/` (Slack/Jira/GitHub/etc.), `notifications/`, `workflow_engine/`, `rules/`, `seer/` (AI), `sentry_apps/`, `digests/`, `services/` (RPC/eventstore).
- `tests/sentry/` — backend tests. `static/app/` + `static/gsApp/` — frontend.
- `src/sentry/models/group*.py` — the issue domain: the core model plus its satellites.

**The core issue model** — the thing users triage/assign/resolve — is `Group` at `src/sentry/models/group.py:704` (`class Group(Model)`, docstring "Aggregated message which summarizes a set of Events", DB table `sentry_groupedmessage`). Its manager is `GroupManager` at `src/sentry/models/group.py:361`. This is a hub symbol: **136 direct callers, ~261 affected symbols across 108 files.**

---

## Step 2 — The issue-model contract

**Core fields** (`src/sentry/models/group.py:711-767`):
- Identity/ownership: `project` FK (`:711`), `short_id` (`:758`), `type` (`:759`), `message`/`culprit` (`:721-724`).
- Triage state (the columns most-read by dependents): `status` (`:727`), `substatus` (`:736`), `priority` (`:762`), `priority_locked_at` (`:763`), `times_seen` (`:746`), `first_seen`/`last_seen`/`active_at` (`:747-752`), `resolved_at` (`:750`), `first_release` FK (`:749`).
- Seer/AI: `seer_fixability_score`, `seer_autofix_last_triggered` (`:764-767`).

**Behavioral contract** dependents rely on (not just columns): `status`/`substatus` are read through helpers `is_resolved()/is_ignored()/is_unresolved()/get_status()` (`:853-860`), `qualified_short_id` (`:842`), `get_absolute_url()`/`get_absolute_api_url()` (`:811-840`), `issue_type`/`issue_category` (`:1200-1204`), and the assignee accessor at `:1170-1176`.

**Satellite models** (where fields are moving to — these are separate tables keyed by `group_id`): `GroupAssignee` (`groupassignee.py`), `GroupResolution` (`groupresolution.py:23`), `GroupInbox` (`groupinbox.py:52`), `GroupShare` (`groupshare.py:17`), `GroupSeen` (`groupseen.py:11`), `GroupOwner` (`groupowner.py`), `GroupRelease` (`grouprelease.py:20`), `GroupMeta` (`groupmeta.py:88`), `GroupOpenPeriod` (`groupopenperiod.py`). `Group` reaches into these itself (e.g. `GroupShare` at `group.py:953`, `GroupAssignee` at `group.py:1170`).

**How references are held/passed — the part a teammate must know:** dependents don't just query `Group`; they **embed a `Group` object as a field inside their own dataclasses/TypedDicts** (composition — see Steps 3/6), and they **pass issue references across the silo boundary as RPC models** (`IssueService`, `RpcGroupShareMetadata`). Any dataclass field typed `group: Group`, any `.group` cached attribute, and any RPC model that flattens issue fields will break when the contract is restructured.

## Step 5 — Blast radius / risk ranking

**Highest risk — subsystems that embed a `Group` in their own persisted/passed contract, so a field move breaks them for every user in that flow:**

1. **`WorkflowEventData` (`workflow_engine/types.py:109`)** — the frozen dataclass threaded through *every* workflow action and data-condition handler (`types.py:308/387`, `processors/delayed_workflow.py:438`). Drives all issue alerts/automation. Tightest coupling: `group: Group` is a required field; a moved field silently changes behavior for all alert delivery.
2. **`GroupInfo` (`event_manager.py:179`)** — carries the freshly-saved `Group` out of ingestion into post-processing/regression logic (`:1694`, `post_process.py:802`). Drives the entire ingest→alert pipeline. Breaks new-issue detection for all incoming events.
3. **`GroupEvent` / `Event.group` (`services/eventstore/models.py:651/690/698`)** — every event-to-issue link. Reads issue fields transiently but is on the hottest path (event serialization). Wide blast.
4. **`BaseNotification.group` (`notifications/base.py:56`)** — embedded on the base class of all issue notifications. A moved `status`/`assignee`/`priority` read breaks Slack/email/MsTeams alerts for everyone.
5. **Issue-index API + serializers (`organization_group_index.py:321`, `activity.py:59`, `group_details.py:380`)** — the issue-stream UI. Reads `status`/`substatus`/`priority`/`times_seen` directly into responses. Breaks the primary triage screen.
6. **RPC boundary (`issues/services/issue/model.py:6/11`, `sentry_apps/services/cell/impl.py:112/195`)** — flattens issue fields into RPC models crossing silos. A contract change here breaks cross-silo (shared issues, sentry-app external issues) with errors that don't reproduce in single-silo tests.
7. **Rules history (`rules/history/base.py:17`, `postgres.py:27`)** and **`RuleGroupHistory`/`WorkflowGroupHistory`** — embed `Group` in aggregation records feeding alert-history UI.

**Lower risk — transient reads, easy to fix:** search executors, snuba utils, summaries tasks, deletion tasks — they query `Group` and read a couple of columns but don't hold the reference in a persisted structure.

---

## Step 6 — Non-obvious dependents (a text search for "Group" would miss or bury these)

- **`src/sentry/digests/utils.py:56** — `_DigestContext` is a `TypedDict` with `counts: Counter[Group]` and `group: Group`. Nothing in "digests" says "issue management," and a `Counter[Group]` keyed by the model uses `Group.__hash__`/`__eq__` — a text grep for `.status`/`group_id` never surfaces it.
- **`src/sentry/tasks/seer/night_shift/models.py:28** — `TriageResult(group: Group)`. Lives deep in an AI "night shift" task tree; name gives no hint it carries the core issue.
- **`src/sentry/workflow_engine/types.py:308 / :387** — `ActionHandler` and `DataConditionHandler` compose `Group` in their type signatures. These are protocol/handler types, not obviously issue code.
- **`src/sentry/notifications/platform/strategies/issue_owners.py:20** — `IssueOwnersActivityAlertStrategy` embeds a `Group`; buried under the notification-platform strategy layer.
- **`src/sentry/services/eventstore/models.py:698** — `GroupEvent` caches its `Group` and `Event.group` (`:651`) resolves it lazily; a grep for the class `Group` won't flag `event.group` call sites.
- **`src/sentry/issues/services/issue/model.py:6/11** — `RpcGroupShareMetadata` / `RpcExternalIssueGroupMetadata` copy issue fields into RPC models. They don't hold a `Group` object at all — they mirror its fields — so field renames break serialization invisibly.
- **`src/sentry/monitors/utils.py:260** (`fetch_associated_groups`), **`src/sentry/web_vitals/issue_platform_adapter.py:122`, **`src/sentry/replays/usecases/replay_counts.py:102** — crons, web-vitals and replays each hold/query an issue far from any "issue" package.

I now have a complete structural picture from the Sense index. Here is the audit, worked step by step.

---

## Step 1 — Orientation

**What this is:** Sentry — a Django (Python backend) + React (TypeScript frontend) error-monitoring platform. ~17k files, ~129k symbols. Not a multi-package monorepo in the npm sense; it's one large Django app plus a React SPA.

**How the code is organized:**
- `src/sentry/` — the backend Django app (~40k symbols). Sub-trees that matter here: `models/` (ORM), `api/` + `issues/endpoints/` (REST layer), `tasks/` (Celery), `integrations/` (Slack/Jira/GitHub/etc.), `notifications/`, `workflow_engine/`, `rules/`, `seer/` (AI), `sentry_apps/`, `digests/`, `services/` (RPC/eventstore).
- `tests/sentry/` — backend tests. `static/app/` + `static/gsApp/` — frontend.
- `src/sentry/models/group*.py` — the issue domain: the core model plus its satellites.

**The core issue model** — the thing users triage/assign/resolve — is `Group` at `src/sentry/models/group.py:704` (`class Group(Model)`, docstring "Aggregated message which summarizes a set of Events", DB table `sentry_groupedmessage`). Its manager is `GroupManager` at `src/sentry/models/group.py:361`. This is a hub symbol: **136 direct callers, ~261 affected symbols across 108 files.**

---

## Step 2 — The issue-model contract

**Core fields** (`src/sentry/models/group.py:711-767`):
- Identity/ownership: `project` FK (`:711`), `short_id` (`:758`), `type` (`:759`), `message`/`culprit` (`:721-724`).
- Triage state (the columns most-read by dependents): `status` (`:727`), `substatus` (`:736`), `priority` (`:762`), `priority_locked_at` (`:763`), `times_seen` (`:746`), `first_seen`/`last_seen`/`active_at` (`:747-752`), `resolved_at` (`:750`), `first_release` FK (`:749`).
- Seer/AI: `seer_fixability_score`, `seer_autofix_last_triggered` (`:764-767`).

**Behavioral contract** dependents rely on (not just columns): `status`/`substatus` are read through helpers `is_resolved()/is_ignored()/is_unresolved()/get_status()` (`:853-860`), `qualified_short_id` (`:842`), `get_absolute_url()`/`get_absolute_api_url()` (`:811-840`), `issue_type`/`issue_category` (`:1200-1204`), and the assignee accessor at `:1170-1176`.

**Satellite models** (where fields are moving to — these are separate tables keyed by `group_id`): `GroupAssignee` (`groupassignee.py`), `GroupResolution` (`groupresolution.py:23`), `GroupInbox` (`groupinbox.py:52`), `GroupShare` (`groupshare.py:17`), `GroupSeen` (`groupseen.py:11`), `GroupOwner` (`groupowner.py`), `GroupRelease` (`grouprelease.py:20`), `GroupMeta` (`groupmeta.py:88`), `GroupOpenPeriod` (`groupopenperiod.py`). `Group` reaches into these itself (e.g. `GroupShare` at `group.py:953`, `GroupAssignee` at `group.py:1170`).

**How references are held/passed — the part a teammate must know:** dependents don't just query `Group`; they **embed a `Group` object as a field inside their own dataclasses/TypedDicts** (composition — see Steps 3/6), and they **pass issue references across the silo boundary as RPC models** (`IssueService`, `RpcGroupShareMetadata`). Any dataclass field typed `group: Group`, any `.group` cached attribute, and any RPC model that flattens issue fields will break when the contract is restructured.

## Step 5 — Blast radius / risk ranking

**Highest risk — subsystems that embed a `Group` in their own persisted/passed contract, so a field move breaks them for every user in that flow:**

1. **`WorkflowEventData` (`workflow_engine/types.py:109`)** — the frozen dataclass threaded through *every* workflow action and data-condition handler (`types.py:308/387`, `processors/delayed_workflow.py:438`). Drives all issue alerts/automation. Tightest coupling: `group: Group` is a required field; a moved field silently changes behavior for all alert delivery.
2. **`GroupInfo` (`event_manager.py:179`)** — carries the freshly-saved `Group` out of ingestion into post-processing/regression logic (`:1694`, `post_process.py:802`). Drives the entire ingest→alert pipeline. Breaks new-issue detection for all incoming events.
3. **`GroupEvent` / `Event.group` (`services/eventstore/models.py:651/690/698`)** — every event-to-issue link. Reads issue fields transiently but is on the hottest path (event serialization). Wide blast.
4. **`BaseNotification.group` (`notifications/base.py:56`)** — embedded on the base class of all issue notifications. A moved `status`/`assignee`/`priority` read breaks Slack/email/MsTeams alerts for everyone.
5. **Issue-index API + serializers (`organization_group_index.py:321`, `activity.py:59`, `group_details.py:380`)** — the issue-stream UI. Reads `status`/`substatus`/`priority`/`times_seen` directly into responses. Breaks the primary triage screen.
6. **RPC boundary (`issues/services/issue/model.py:6/11`, `sentry_apps/services/cell/impl.py:112/195`)** — flattens issue fields into RPC models crossing silos. A contract change here breaks cross-silo (shared issues, sentry-app external issues) with errors that don't reproduce in single-silo tests.
7. **Rules history (`rules/history/base.py:17`, `postgres.py:27`)** and **`RuleGroupHistory`/`WorkflowGroupHistory`** — embed `Group` in aggregation records feeding alert-history UI.

**Lower risk — transient reads, easy to fix:** search executors, snuba utils, summaries tasks, deletion tasks — they query `Group` and read a couple of columns but don't hold the reference in a persisted structure.

## Step 6 — Non-obvious dependents (a text search for "Group" would miss or bury these)

- **`src/sentry/digests/utils.py:56** — `_DigestContext` is a `TypedDict` with `counts: Counter[Group]` and `group: Group`. Nothing in "digests" says "issue management," and a `Counter[Group]` keyed by the model uses `Group.__hash__`/`__eq__` — a text grep for `.status`/`group_id` never surfaces it.
- **`src/sentry/tasks/seer/night_shift/models.py:28** — `TriageResult(group: Group)`. Lives deep in an AI "night shift" task tree; name gives no hint it carries the core issue.
- **`src/sentry/workflow_engine/types.py:308 / :387** — `ActionHandler` and `DataConditionHandler` compose `Group` in their type signatures. These are protocol/handler types, not obviously issue code.
- **`src/sentry/notifications/platform/strategies/issue_owners.py:20** — `IssueOwnersActivityAlertStrategy` embeds a `Group`; buried under the notification-platform strategy layer.
- **`src/sentry/services/eventstore/models.py:698** — `GroupEvent` caches its `Group` and `Event.group` (`:651`) resolves it lazily; a grep for the class `Group` won't flag `event.group` call sites.
- **`src/sentry/issues/services/issue/model.py:6/11** — `RpcGroupShareMetadata` / `RpcExternalIssueGroupMetadata` copy issue fields into RPC models. They don't hold a `Group` object at all — they mirror its fields — so field renames break serialization invisibly.
- **`src/sentry/monitors/utils.py:260** (`fetch_associated_groups`), **`src/sentry/web_vitals/issue_platform_adapter.py:122`, **`src/sentry/replays/usecases/replay_counts.py:102** — crons, web-vitals and replays each hold/query an issue far from any "issue" package.
- **`src/sentry/utils/groupreference.py:46** (`find_referenced_groups`) — parses commit messages for issue references; a utility no one associates with the model's contract.
- **`static/app/views/sharedGroupDetails/index.tsx:23** / **`static/gsAdmin/views/debuggingTools.tsx:23** — frontend TS response types that must stay in lockstep with the backend contract but live in a different language/tree.
